panels.description: Per-project LLM spend, rate of vornik_executor_llm_cost_usd_total. This is the billing view — what each project is actually paying.
panels.title: Spend Rate by Project (USD/hour)

panels.description: Effective cost per successful step by role+model. Factors in failure rate: a cheap-but-flaky model costs more per useful outcome than its list price suggests.

targets.expr: sum by (role, model) (rate(vornik_executor_model_cost_usd_total[1h])) / clamp_min(sum by (role, model) (rate(vornik_executor_agent_step_outcomes_total{outcome="ok"}[1h])), 1e-12)

panels.title: Effective $/Success by Role+Model

panels.description: Token usage stacked by direction (prompt vs completion). Flattening periods indicate the model's steady-state draw; spikes trace expensive tool-loop tasks.
panels.title: LLM Tokens Rate (by direction)

panels.description: Agent step outcomes. Sustained failure or timeout rates point at a flaky role+model combo to review on the Effective $/Success panel.
panels.title: Agent Step Outcomes

panels.description: Tool-calling iterations per agent step. Spikes here often indicate runaway loops driving up cost before the budget cap trips.
panels.title: Iterations Rate by Role+Model

panels.description: Lifetime success rate per role+model from vornik_executor_model_success_rate. Cancelled runs are excluded from the denominator. Low values here combined with high effective-cost identify models that should be swapped out.
panels.title: Success Rate by Role+Model

panels.description: Lifetime cost ÷ successful step count per role+model from vornik_executor_model_effective_cost_usd. This is $/useful-outcome, not $/attempt. Compare across models running the same role to see which gives the best $ per success.
panels.title: Effective $/Success by Role+Model

panels.description: Ranked model performance leaderboard. Columns: success rate (higher is better), effective $/success (lower is better), lifetime cost. Sort the effective-cost column to find the cheapest-per-success combos; the success-rate column tells you whether cheap = adequate.
panels.title: Model Performance Leaderboard
Resolver Decisions (short_circuit vs llm)
panels.description: Operators tune the embedding/Levenshtein gate by watching the short_circuit ratio — it's the single biggest cost lever.

panels.title: KG Stage Token Rate
panels.description: Per-stage LLM token consumption. Cost dashboards multiply this by the per-model rate.

panels.title: KG Quality & Recovery Counters
panels.description: validator drops = below-threshold edges. same-chunk dedup = dup canonical_name in one chunk. cross-chunk recovered = ErrDuplicateKey path on Insert. circuit trips should stay flat.

panels.title: Safety Event Rate by Kind
panels.description: Per-kind rate over the last 5m. A sustained breaker_trip line means the strategy is repeatedly hitting drawdown — investigate before the operator notices missing fills.

panels.title: Orders Ingested (placed vs refused)
panels.description: Refused orders are dropped at the envelope (cap / kill / breaker); placed orders made it to the venue.

panels.title: Ingest Errors (validation, auth, persist)
panels.description: Non-zero here means the broker is sending malformed payloads or losing auth — audit channel itself has a bug, not the trading flow.

panels.title: Evaluation Outcomes (ok / error / skipped / abstain_no_config)
panels.description: The gap between `ok` and `verdicts_total` rate equals `error+abstain_no_config+skipped` — lets you see judge healthiness at metric time.

panels.title: Judge Confidence Distribution (p50/p95)
panels.description: Persistently low confidence usually means the rubric is too vague for the task type, or the judge model is undersized.

panels.title: Signal Rate by Detector
panels.description: A new detector suddenly dominating usually means the model started hallucinating in a new shape — worth a glance at recent prompts.

panels.title: Upserts by Domain & Evidence by Polarity
